channel.title: State-Policy Think-Tank Digest
channel.description: Latest posts from free-market policy institutes across the U.S.

item.title: [Empowerms] Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again?
item.description:
Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? On this episode of the Empower Podcast, State Rep. Otis Anthony joins Grant for a wide-ranging conversation about poverty, work, education, economic development, and the future of the Mississippi Delta. Anthony shares how growing up in Indianola shaped his view of public service, why he believes the Delta’s…

The post Rep. Otis Anthony: Can the Delta Rise Again? first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Libertas] Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families
item.description:
Libertas Network’s acquisition of Praxis ignites a bold revolution in education by empowering young innovators to bypass outdated systems and chart their own path to success.

The post Libertas Network Acquires Family Brand to Strengthen Intentional Families appeared first on Libertas Network: Changing Hearts, Minds, and Laws.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying
item.description:
We hear it all too often from In-Home Support Services (IHSS) care providers considering opting out of their union: “If I do, I’ll lose my dental insurance.”

The post CALIFORNIA: IHSS providers tricked into staying, overpaying appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF President discusses AI data centers on “Powering America” podcast
item.description:
In the latest episode of the Powering America Podcast, Paul Gessing, president of the Rio Grande Foundation, discusses data centers and energy policy in New Mexico. Gessing highlights the state’s potential for data center development due to its natural resources and stable environment, while also addressing the challenges posed by political opposition and environmental concerns. The […]

The post RGF President discusses AI data centers on “Powering America” podcast appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine Taxpayers Are Once Again Paying for Bellows’ Fight with Trump
item.description:
After a controversial Bureau of Motor Vehicles decision, the Maine taxpayers are once again being dragged into a high-profile constitutional fight with the Trump administration. The U.S. Department of Justice has sued the State of Maine, Governor Janet Mills, Attorney General Aaron Frey, and Secretary of State Shenna Bellows over a Bureau of Motor Vehicles […]

The post Maine Taxpayers Are Once Again Paying for Bellows’ Fight with Trump appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Freedom Foundation hails Louisiana’s new worker-protection bill
item.description:
The Freedom Foundation today applauded the Louisiana Legislature for passing Senate Bill 312, a measure that strengthens worker freedom by ensuring employees understand their rights before joining a union. 

The post Freedom Foundation hails Louisiana’s new worker-protection bill appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] The Bottom-Up Case for Regulating AI Chatbots
item.description:
When looking to regulate emerging technologies, the popular instinct has been to draft these rules from the top...

The post The Bottom-Up Case for Regulating AI Chatbots appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Beacontn] First Principles Series: Don’t Ban Data Centers. Don’t Subsidize Them, Either.
item.description: We can allow data centers to exist while keeping the courts open for nuisance lawsuits against bad actors who infringe upon protections like water rights when other property owners are actually harmed by them.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Unconstitutional Programs and the Administrative State: Stopping the 1419 Project
item.description: Over the past century, political progressives have built a bureaucratic machine with the goal of undermining our republic. It accomplishes this by making people dependent on government programs and by ignoring the will of the people and their elected representatives, ruling solely through executive fiat. Idaho has long been at the forefront of the battle […]

item.title: [Jbartlett] BET cuts for the long run
item.description:
As the 2026 legislative session wound down, it looked as though Republicans wouldn’t be able to agree on one of the party’s signature issues: cutting business taxes. During committee of conference negotiations last week, word spread (and was even reported in the news) that a House-passed Business Enterprise Tax cut was dead.  House and Senate […]

The post BET cuts for the long run appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Rifreedom] Un-Affordability: Speaker Blazejewski’s Budget Denies Science, Will Raise Energy Costs for Ocean Staters … but Center Claims One Major Victory
item.description:
Despite common-sense energy reforms suggested by Governor McKee; despite pledged reforms by all other New England states; and despite indisputable research that shows how Rhode Island’s net zero emissions energy strategy will lead to unaffordable costs and dangerous risks … the budget put forth by the new Speaker of the RI House of Representative, Christopher Blazejewski … doubles-down on the state’s radical Act On Climate mandates.

The post Un-Affordability: Speaker Blazejewski’s Budget Denies Science, Will Raise Energy Costs for Ocean Staters … but Center Claims One Major Victory appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Property Tax Relief Options Explained
item.description: The most important point to understand regarding property taxes is that they are budget-driven. This simply means local taxing districts, which include counties, cities, schools, fire districts, etc., set property tax budgets, and those budgets are the primary driver of property tax payments and the changes (usually increases) over time. Long-term property tax relief requires […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Circuit Court – Adams County
item.description:
Circuit Court – Adams County

The post Circuit Court – Adams County appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Idahofreedom] Coverage vs. Care (Part 1)
item.description: On Valentine’s Day 1966, Gov. Bob Smylie called a special session of the state Legislature to deal, primarily, with reapportioning the Idaho Senate to comply with the U.S. Supreme Court’s one-man-one-vote requirement. Back then, there was a senator for each of the state’s 44 counties, and the courts had ruled the arrangement unconstitutional. So when […]

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells’ $368,885 Secret Meeting
item.description:
For Immediate Release: June 10, 2026 Contact: press@reforminggovernment.org   IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells $368,885 Secret Meeting Lawsuit argues DPI violated open meetings laws, seeks to invalidate DPI’s secret meeting that weakened Wisconsin testing standards Delafield, Wis. — The Institute for Reforming Government (IRG), represented by the Wisconsin Transparency Project, is taking the Department […]

The post IRG Sues DPI Over Wisconsin Dells’ $368,885 Secret Meeting appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Reforminggovernment] Jake Curtis | Point Taken
item.description: The post Jake Curtis | Point Taken appeared first on Institute for Reforming Government.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Maine’s 2026 Primary Shows Why RCV Should Be Repealed
item.description:
Maine voters went to the polls on June 9, but in several of the state’s most important primary races, Election Day did not actually produce a winner. Instead, both gubernatorial primaries and the Democratic primary in Maine’s 2nd Congressional District are headed into ranked-choice voting tabulation. As a result, Mainers are left waiting days after […]

The post Maine’s 2026 Primary Shows Why RCV Should Be Repealed appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Facts to help you decide whether Wisconsin children should be eligible for donor-funded education scholarships
item.description:
What Wisconsinites need to know about how the Federal Scholarship Tax Credit program works.

The post Facts to help you decide whether Wisconsin children should be eligible for donor-funded education scholarships appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] Testimony on PPS’ Racial Educational Equity Policy (Policy 2.10.010-P)
item.description:
By John A. Charles, Jr. June 8, 2026 RE: Policy 2.10.010-P Dear Board...

The post Testimony on PPS’ Racial Educational Equity Policy (Policy 2.10.010-P) appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Cascadepolicy] PPS’ Failed Equity Policy: No Evidence, No Results
item.description:
By Naomi InmanTestimony by John A. Charles, Jr. Portland Public Schools briefly revisited...

The post PPS’ Failed Equity Policy: No Evidence, No Results appeared first on Cascade Policy Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Welfare Cards Used in Hawaii, Guam and Across the Country, Records Show
item.description:
Connecticut electronic benefit cards (EBT) were used in more than $7.2 million in taxpayer-funded cash transactions outside the state over a roughly two-and-a-half-year period, including in Florida resort towns, Hawaii […]

The post Connecticut Welfare Cards Used in Hawaii, Guam and Across the Country, Records Show appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Jbartlett] Does Jeff Bezos benefit from a NH Business Enterprise Tax cut?
item.description:
New Hampshire’s unique state tax structure is hard to understand, especially for people who don’t own a business.  More than a third (36%) of the state General Fund and Education Trust Fund revenue comes from business taxes.  The vast majority of that comes from the Business Profits Tax (BPT), but the lesser-known Business Enterprise Tax […]

The post Does Jeff Bezos benefit from a NH Business Enterprise Tax cut? appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] The Bear Flag and California’s history blind spot
item.description:
June 14 is the anniversary of the 1846 Bear Flag Revolt that helped set California on the path to statehood. But how many California students could explain what the Bear Flag Republic was, why it mattered, or how California became a state? The truth is, we have no idea. California schools are expected to teach...

The post The Bear Flag and California’s history blind spot first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Wealthiest taxpayers pay more than their fair share of income taxes
item.description: I recently was talking with someone who said wealthy people should pay their fair share of income taxes, and I asked, “What do you mean by ‘fair share ‘?” He struggled with the question, so I said, “Do you mean that a group of people with 50% of all the income should pay 50% of […]

item.title: [Empirecenter] It Is Time to Rethink the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative
item.description:
Before budget negotiations, Gov. Hochul warned that unless New York changes its climate plans, New Yorkers could face a $2.26-per-gallon....

The post It Is Time to Rethink the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] Opinion piece: Report on economic development incentives is incomplete
item.description:
The following appeared in the Santa Fe New Mexican on June 6, 2026. It appeared in other papers across the state. Recently the Legislative Finance Committee produced a report in which (to summarize the findings) they made the case that New Mexico “spends” over half-a-billion dollars on tax breaks that generated little economic return for […]

The post Opinion piece: Report on economic development incentives is incomplete appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Freedomfoundation] Arizona voters to decide on measure curtailing taxpayer support for teachers’ unions
item.description:
The Arizona Senate today passed House Concurrent Resolution 2040, a Freedom Foundation-backed measure that would prohibit public school districts from using taxpayer resources to support union organizing activities.

The post Arizona voters to decide on measure curtailing taxpayer support for teachers’ unions appeared first on Freedom Foundation.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] For the Children? Data Center Strengthens Local School District, Community
item.description:
Richland Parish, Louisiana is not often the subject of national news. This month has been an exception. The Wall Street Journal, among other high-profile media outlets, reported on “teachers getting $50,000 bonuses thanks to a massive meta data center.” The astonishing numbers don’t stop with the teacher bonuses. The Journal went on to report, “The...

The post For the Children? Data Center Strengthens Local School District, Community appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Empowerms] Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change
item.description:
Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change For years, Empower Mississippi has focused on removing external barriers to opportunity. But what if some of the barriers holding people back aren’t external at all? In this episode, Grant sits down with licensed professional counselor Dr. Brandon Henry to explore the internal obstacles that often prevent people from…

The post Dr. Branden Henry: How People Change first appeared on Empower Mississippi.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Has a Small-Home Shortage; Zoning Reform Can Help Fix It
item.description:
Boston – A new Pioneer Institute report finds that a mismatch between who lives in Massachusetts and the housing being built is squeezing two generations at once: young families who cannot get into a house, and seniors who cannot downsize.

Local land use rules often make it difficult to build small, reasonably priced, and low-maintenance homes, despite a growing need for them as the population ages and average household sizes continue to shrink.

The post Massachusetts Has a Small-Home Shortage; Zoning Reform Can Help Fix It appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraskanomics: Mark McHargue on Nebraska’s Agriculture Industry
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of Nebraskanomics as we welcome President of the Nebraska Farm Bureau, Mark McHargue. During the episode Mark and Platte CEO Jim Vokal discuss the impact of agriculture in Nebraska, next steps on property tax reform, and what the future looks like for our state’s largest industry.  Listen now on...

The post Nebraskanomics: Mark McHargue on Nebraska’s Agriculture Industry appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Rural Hospitals Need Prosperity
item.description: Kansas now has more rural hospitals at immediate risk of closure than any other state, according to KAKE News. That is serious. Rural healthcare access matters. Families should not have to drive for hours to access emergency care. But if Kansas treats this only as a hospital funding problem, it will miss the real issue. […]

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] Massachusetts Has a New Graduation Framework. It Still Needs a Stronger Accountability System
item.description:
Today's ruling is a serious disappointment for Massachusetts families struggling under one of the highest tax and cost burdens in the nation. The Court's decision rests on a drafting error in the Attorney General's summary of the petition—a matter entirely outside the control of voters and petition signers. As a result, the people of Massachusetts will be denied the opportunity to decide whether the Commonwealth should reduce the income tax rate from 5 percent to 4 percent.

The post Massachusetts Has a New Graduation Framework. It Still Needs a Stronger Accountability System appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] How Much Flow Preserves the Delta?
item.description:
When choosing what policies and projects might best ensure abundant water for California’s ecosystems, farms, and cities, the role of the delta is central. Even if large scale desalination were to someday deliver over a million acre feet of water per year to California’s coastal cities, how we manage the delta affects many times that...

The post How Much Flow Preserves the Delta? first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] A Pyrrhic Bargain on AI Preemption
item.description:
The White House and Congress are reportedly working on a deal to trade free expression online for free...

The post A Pyrrhic Bargain on AI Preemption appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Riograndefoundation] RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory
item.description:
This post will be a  lot less policy oriented than normal. That’s because as the head of RGF, this week has been VERY busy. It started with a HUGE win at ABQ City Council on Monday night when on a 5-4 vote Council (again) rejected a major tax increase. The vote was 5-4 against raising […]

The post RGF on the road/post mortem on ABQ City Council victory appeared first on Rio Grande Foundation.

item.title: [Will Law] WILL Launches The Wisconsin Education Comeback: A Roadmap for Student Success
item.description:
WILL has launched “The Wisconsin Education Comeback: A Roadmap for Student Success” a series of reports to reform education in Wisconsin. The roadmap will aim to provide a comprehensive set of K-12 education reforms for the next Governor and legislature to tackle.

The post WILL Launches The Wisconsin Education Comeback: A Roadmap for Student Success appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Empirecenter] 34 MTA Workers Made $200K+ In Overtime In 2025
item.description:
Thirty-four employees of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) received more than $200,000 in overtime payments in 2025, as total annual....

The post 34 MTA Workers Made $200K+ In Overtime In 2025 appeared first on Empire Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pioneerinstitute] MA Voters Denied Voice On State Income Tax Reduction
item.description:
Today’s ruling by the Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court is a serious disappointment for Commonwealth families struggling under one of the highest tax and cost burdens in the nation. The Court’s decision rests on a drafting error in the Attorney General’s summary of...

The post MA Voters Denied Voice On State Income Tax Reduction appeared first on Pioneer Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Wisconsin Supreme Court Sides with WILL in Significant Legal Victory to End DEI
item.description:
WILL secured a significant victory for true equality under the law at the Wisconsin Supreme Court. The Court ruled that the racially discriminatory “Minority Undergraduate Retention Program” was unconstitutional and that taxpayers have standing in court to challenge such programs.

The post Wisconsin Supreme Court Sides with WILL in Significant Legal Victory to End DEI appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Californiapolicycenter] Subsidizing the Visible Hand: California’s Departure from 1776
item.description:
As the United States celebrates the semiquincentennial of its founding in 1776, it is worth reflecting on a remarkable historical coincidence: the birth of our nation occurred the same year Adam Smith published An Inquiry into the Nature and Causes of the Wealth of Nations.  Smith’s seminal work articulated the concept of the invisible hand,...

The post Subsidizing the Visible Hand: California’s Departure from 1776 first appeared on California Policy Center.

item.title: [Jamesmadison] Florida Politics: One Label, One Standard
item.description:
Americans have a right to know what is in the food they buy. They also deserve a system...

The post Florida Politics: One Label, One Standard appeared first on James Madison Institute.

item.title: [Foropportunity] When a Love Story Meets the Tax Code
item.description:
Key Points The Cost of Combining Incomes The Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) is one of our country’s largest anti-poverty initiatives. The […]

The post When a Love Story Meets the Tax Code appeared first on For Opportunity.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Six Stages of AI
item.description:
When something new comes along, everyone suddenly has to have an opinion about it. Often the same one. When crypto went mainstream, people who had never owned so much as a share were suddenly holding forth on bitcoin. When Ozempic arrived, people who had never once expressed a view on weight loss were just as […]

The post The Six Stages of AI appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] It’s Called Soccer
item.description:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is underway across North America. Soccer fans from all over the world have descended on cities like Boston, Los Angeles and Kansas City to watch their national teams participate in the beautiful game. With all of this going on we have seen something incredible, the rest of the world discovering...

The post It’s Called Soccer first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Data centers often bring faster connections to world
item.description:
Geography matters for what’s called “latency,” the small but often important amount of time it takes signals to travel from a user to whatever data center holds the information he’s using.

The post Data centers often bring faster connections to world appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Badgerinstitute] Wisconsin should choose the right side of the income tax divide
item.description:
The choice Wisconsin leaders make about income tax policy will have important implications for the state’s future competitiveness and prosperity.

The post Wisconsin should choose the right side of the income tax divide appeared first on Badger Institute.

item.title: [Mackinac] Ben Sasse’s farewell address
item.description: ‘Turn off the devices, break bread together’

item.description:

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Connecticut Keeps Discovering the Same Credit Card Scandal. Here’s How to Stop the Next One.
item.description:
Purchasing-card (P-Card) controversies have surfaced across Connecticut government for years. The names and amounts change; the pattern does not. Receipts go missing. Personal or unauthorized purchases slip through. Supervisors approve […]

The post Connecticut Keeps Discovering the Same Credit Card Scandal. Here’s How to Stop the Next One.  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Juneteenth on America’s 250th
item.description:
Juneteenth marks one of the great turning points in American history: the day when the promise of 1776 was carried..

item.description:
Risk is part of the game for any business launching a profit-seeking venture—and taxpayers should never be forced to subsidize..

item.description:
By Vanessa Mayerwith Naomi Inman Hunting, fishing, farming, and ranching have always been...

item.title: [Jbartlett] NH fishing & hunting license fees are among New England’s highest
item.description:
At the request of Gov. Kelly Ayotte, the New Hampshire Fish & Game Department has paused a proposed increase in fishing and hunting license fees.  Department officials say the increases are needed to shore up the budget. The last fee increase took effect in 2016.  A decade’s worth of inflation has eroded the value of […]

The post NH fishing & hunting license fees are among New England’s highest appeared first on THE JOSIAH BARTLETT CENTER FOR PUBLIC POLICY.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Nebraska Population Growth: Are We Winning the Race for Residents?
item.description:
When measuring a state’s economic vitality, the most honest metric isn’t always in a government budget report—it’s in a moving van. Demographics are the bedrock of our workforce, tax base, and future prosperity. If we want to evaluate how Nebraska is truly performing, we have to look at whether we are attracting new talent or...

The post Nebraska Population Growth: Are We Winning the Race for Residents? appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.description:
The Trump administration’s latest salvo against Medicaid fraud takes aim at a different kind of target – two high-ranking New....

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Sutherland Institute joins amicus brief in Rio Grande Foundation v. Oliver
item.description:
This law unconstitutionally infringes Petitioner’s right to free association and speech.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Pelican’s Summer Reading List 2026: For the Beach, the Porch, or Wherever Summer Takes You
item.description:
Summer offers something that’s often in short supply during the rest of the year: time to think. This year’s Pelican Institute Summer Reading List explores the ideas, institutions, and communities that shape American life. From civic engagement and economic literacy to leadership, history, and emerging technology, these recommendations offer something for readers of every age...

The post Pelican’s Summer Reading List 2026: For the Beach, the Porch, or Wherever Summer Takes You appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Maui County should allow two ADUs on all residential lots
item.description:
June 19, 2026, 9 a.m. Kalana O Maui Building To: Maui County Council Alice Lee, Chair Yuki Lei Sugimura, Vice Chair From: Jonathan Helton, Policy Analyst Grassroot Institute of Hawaii RE: Bill 78, CD1 (2026) — ON ACCESSORY DWELLINGS ON RESIDENTIALLY ZONED LOTS Aloha Chair Lee, Vice Chair Sugimura and other members of the Council, […]

The post Maui County should allow two ADUs on all residential lots appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] On Father’s Day, A Reflection on Abiding Support
item.description:
With Father’s Day right around the corner, I find myself once again reflecting on the love, care, and guidance I’ve been shown by father over the 27 years of my life. I certainly would not be who I am now without his unconditional and unceasing concern and constant desire to ensure my well-being and good,...

The post On Father’s Day, A Reflection on Abiding Support first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Class of 2026: Make yourself indispensable
item.description: Will automation make creativity irrelevant?

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Poll: Taxes, economy top concerns for Illinoisans
item.description:
A recent survey also shows that fully half of residents would move out of the state given the opportunity.

The post Poll: Taxes, economy top concerns for Illinoisans  appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Mspolicy] The Federal Scholarship Tax Credit – What You Need to Know
item.description:
Let me introduce you to three letters you are going to hear a great deal about in the months ahead: SGO. If they mean nothing to you today, they soon will. SGO stands for Scholarship Granting Organization — and thanks to a change in federal law, Mississippi is about to see a whole network of […]

The post The Federal Scholarship Tax Credit – What You Need to Know appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Mainepolicy] Portland Study Reinforces What We Already Know: Inclusionary Zoning is Terrible Policy
item.description:
For years, Maine Policy Institute has warned that Portland’s inclusionary zoning policy would make the city’s housing shortage worse, not better. Last December, when Portland commissioned yet another study on the subject, MPI noted that the evidence was already clear: Inclusionary zoning acts like a tax on housing development, raises the cost of building, and […]

The post Portland Study Reinforces What We Already Know: Inclusionary Zoning is Terrible Policy appeared first on Maine Policy Institute.

item.title: [Goldwaterinstitute] Arizona Enacts New Law Protecting Businesses That Call 911
item.description:
When an emergency occurs, every second counts. The last thing anyone should have to consider during a crisis is whether..

The post Arizona Enacts New Law Protecting Businesses That Call 911 appeared first on Goldwater Institute.

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Before New Jersey Becomes the Next Climate Superfund Test Case, Let’s See What the Courts Say
item.description:
By Wells Winegar The debate surrounding New Jersey’s proposed Climate Superfund Act has largely centered on climate policy. Supporters argue […]

The post Before New Jersey Becomes the Next Climate Superfund Test Case, Let’s See What the Courts Say first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Will Law] Judge Blocks Milwaukee’s Food Truck Ordinance Following WILL’s Lawsuit
item.description:
A Milwaukee County Circuit Court Judge blocked enforcement of Milwaukee’s 10:00 p.m. food truck curfew while the case continues through litigation. The order follows a lawsuit filed by WILL on behalf of The Fatty Patty food truck and its owner, Abdallah Ismail.

The post Judge Blocks Milwaukee’s Food Truck Ordinance Following WILL’s Lawsuit appeared first on Wisconsin Institute for Law & Liberty.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] No, Proving Medical Frailty Isn’t Too Much to Ask
item.description:
The Trump administration just released a new rule making clear that Medicaid’s work requirements are just that — requirements. Beginning in 2027, able-bodied adults who gained Medicaid coverage through Obamacare’s expansion of the program will generally need to spend at least 80 hours per month working, studying, participating in job training, or engaging in community […]

The post No, Proving Medical Frailty Isn’t Too Much to Ask appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] Declining Obamacare enrollment isn’t the crisis critics claim
item.description:
Enrollment in Obamacare’s exchange is falling, according to a new analysis from the Commonwealth Fund. The study suggests that the expiration of the enhanced premium subsidies enacted during the pandemic has made coverage unaffordable for many Americans. Some consumers undoubtedly have decided that exchange plans are no longer worth the cost. According to a report […]

The post Declining Obamacare enrollment isn’t the crisis critics claim appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Yankeeinstitute] Three Energy Plans, One Very Expensive Problem
item.description:
Connecticut’s gubernatorial race is producing three distinct approaches to one of the state’s most expensive problems: electricity.  Gov. Ned Lamont focuses on utility accountability. Democratic challenger Josh Elliott targetsmonopoly control […]

The post Three Energy Plans, One Very Expensive Problem  appeared first on Yankee Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] New study: When taxes are on the ballot, more voters should have a say
item.description: The study, Tax increase ballot measures: How to increase voter participation, authored by Policy Fellow Sam Cardwell, finds that local tax increases are frequently decided during special elections that attract significantly fewer voters than primary or general elections.

item.title: [Kansaspolicy] Affordability issue #1: Property tax increases far exceed most Kansans’ change in personal income
item.description: The #1 affordability issue facing many Kansans is property tax increases that are literally taxing some people out of their homes and prompting some small businesses to close. The average taxable assessed home value jumped 40% over the last four years, resulting in a 31% average property tax increase. Meanwhile, real (inflation-adjusted) per capita personal […]

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Fraud tour begins: Follow the money, connect the dots
item.description:
Center of the American Experiment announced a summer tour of events featuring an hour-long program about Minnesota’s historic fraud scandal that made national headlines and continues to produce indictments and convictions to this day. The program is led by American Experiment Policy Fellow Bill Glahn, who’s been exposing fraud in Minnesota state government programs for...

The post Fraud tour begins: Follow the money, connect the dots appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Celebrate two anniversaries this year
item.description:
This is a year of celebration — both for our country and the Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.   Almost 250 years ago, on July 4, 1776, the Continental Congress approved the Declaration of Independence. That revolutionary document gave birth to the United States of America, and has shaped hearts and minds around the world ever […]

The post Celebrate two anniversaries this year appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] Missouri Missed an Opportunity on Reading Reform
item.description:
House Bill (HB) 2872, which contained important early literacy reforms, was on the move during the 2026 Missouri legislative session, but did not ultimately become law. If passed, HB 2872 […]

The post Missouri Missed an Opportunity on Reading Reform appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Rifreedom] ONE SCORE, Twenty Failures: The High Cost of Rhode Island’s One-Party Rule
item.description:
Rhode Island's twenty year single-party rule has driven sharp decline. The new “One Score” report details twenty major policy failures in taxes, affordability, education, business climate & more. Read it now.

The post ONE SCORE, Twenty Failures: The High Cost of Rhode Island’s One-Party Rule appeared first on RI Center for Freedom and Prosperity.

item.title: [Pacificresearch] California’s Regulatory Blob
item.description:
On the surface, it might appear to be minor. Nothing too big. But from seemingly insignificant rules come sweeping, burdensome regulatory frameworks. The plan is to only outlaw a particular type of automobile tire for fuel economy purposes, but it sets up regulators to police anything they wish to, from engine displacement (while internal-combustion engines are still […]

The post California’s Regulatory Blob appeared first on Pacific Research Institute.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Illinois lawmakers finally pass bill to end home equity theft
item.description:
The state finally passed legislation to comply with a 2023 U.S. Supreme Court ruling that the government may not keep surplus equity when property is sold for unpaid taxes.

The post Illinois lawmakers finally pass bill to end home equity theft appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Illinoispolicy] Lawmakers boost their salaries to over $100,000
item.description:
Their base pay has risen almost 50% since J.B. Pritzker became governor, compared with just a 30% increase for Illinoisans.

The post Lawmakers boost their salaries to over $100,000 appeared first on Illinois Policy.

item.title: [Texaspolicy] A Great Debate on the Great Books
item.description:
Recently, a strange, mercurial community of self-described, sometimes externally attributed, “conservative” academics engaged in a wide-ranging discussion of the “Great Books.” Luke Burgis, author and founder of the Cluny Institute, set off the tweetstorm with what on the surface seemed like a simple question: “What is the strongest argument from the political Right AGAINST Great...

The post A Great Debate on the Great Books first appeared on Texas Public Policy Foundation.

item.title: [Mackinac] Michigan should embrace economic freedom
item.description: It’s good and also associated with increased job, population numbers

item.title: [Newjerseypolicyinstitute] Governor Sherrill is Targeting Junk Fees, but Where Do Government Fees Stand?
item.description:
by Megan Whitman NJPI Junior Policy Fellow   Governor Sherrill recently signed an Executive Order targeting “junk fees,” fees that […]

The post Governor Sherrill is Targeting Junk Fees, but Where Do Government Fees Stand? first appeared on New Jersey Policy Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] The Declaration of Independence: Its debt to history and meaning
item.description: Governments have only those powers that the people, by consent, have given them. When a government no longer serves its rightful purpose, the people may reorganize or remove it.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] What If You Eliminated Personal Property Taxes and Nobody Noticed?
item.description:
Listen to this article There is a lot of ongoing discussion about eliminating personal property taxes. There have been bills introduced to eliminate them. It’s a major topic of debate […]

The post What If You Eliminated Personal Property Taxes and Nobody Noticed? appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] The U.S. needs a new safety net | Angela Rachidi
item.description:
AEI Senior Fellow Angela Rachidi joins host Nic Dunn to discuss benefit cliffs, the Upward Mobility Act, and whether state-led innovation could help more families achieve self-reliance through things like empowerment accounts.

The post The U.S. needs a new safety net | Angela Rachidi appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Showmeinstitute] The Continued Growth of the Four-Day School Week in Missouri
item.description:
The Missouri Department of Elementary and Secondary Education (DESE) reports that 188 of 518 school districts will be operating on a four-day school week (4dsw) during the upcoming school year. […]

The post The Continued Growth of the Four-Day School Week in Missouri appeared first on Show-Me Institute.

item.title: [Mountainstatespolicy] Analysis: Montana cut income tax rates, and revenues more than doubled
item.description: Over the past decade, individual income tax collections increased from $1.06 billion in Fiscal Year 2014 to $2.24 billion in Fiscal Year 2024. That's an increase of more than 111 percent.

item.title: [Platteinstitute] Where Nebraska Stands on Childcare Regulations in 2026
item.description:
Nebraska has a lot to be proud of in the Archbridge Institute’s 2026 State Childcare Regulations Index. Ranking a solid 10th nationwide with a childcare freedom score of 5.48 out of 10, the Cornhusker State stands as a competitive leader in keeping childcare regulations manageable. Within the West North Central region, Nebraska ranks 2nd, demonstrating...

The post Where Nebraska Stands on Childcare Regulations in 2026 appeared first on Platte Institute.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] What voters should know about Minnesota’s school funding amendment
item.description:
This November, Minnesota voters will weigh in on a constitutional amendment that would change how the state’s Permanent School Fund gets distributed to schools. Because the fund is not widely discussed, here is how it works and what the proposed amendment would change.  Permanent School Fund When Minnesota became a state in 1858, the federal...

The post What voters should know about Minnesota’s school funding amendment appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Mspolicy] Government pays to lobby government — with your tax dollars
item.description:
Across Mississippi, government bodies spend public money hiring lobbyists — to lobby government. I would not have believed quite how much of it goes on until we built the tool that lays it bare. Let me explain how we got here. Within days of entering the White House in January 2025, President Trump set up […]

The post Government pays to lobby government — with your tax dollars appeared first on Mississippi Center for Public Policy.

item.title: [Pelicanpolicy] Just a Bill? Two Troubling Technology Bills Are Heating Up This Summer
item.description:
As the temperatures rise, so too does a distinctly anti-innovation, anti-competition sentiment in the United States Congress. Cloaked in the language of “protecting consumers” and “giving the public a role,” recently introduced bills create regulatory regimes that would dismantle consumer choice and cripple American industries.  The American Innovation and Choice Online Act (AICOA) is deceptively...

The post Just a Bill? Two Troubling Technology Bills Are Heating Up This Summer appeared first on Pelican Policy.

item.title: [Americanexperiment] Bathhouses, Dog Parks, and CHAOS: Inside Minneapolis’ BROKEN Priorities
item.description:
Minneapolis is struggling while leaders focus on bathhouses and dog parks, the state’s Clemency Review Commission seems a little too eager to let convicted killers walk free, and the Social Security fund is finally about to run out of money (here’s how Minnesotans will be affected). Later, economist Martha Njolomole shares how our state’s policymakers...

The post Bathhouses, Dog Parks, and CHAOS: Inside Minneapolis’ BROKEN Priorities appeared first on American Experiment.

item.title: [Sutherlandinstitute] Virtual learning: From online schools to AI tools
item.description:
As Utah policymakers navigate education technology, they should be guided by a simple principle: technology should expand educational opportunity for families while being evaluated honestly for its results on student learning.

The post Virtual learning: From online schools to AI tools appeared first on Sutherland Institute.

item.title: [Grassrootinstitute] Allow affordable housing buyers to immediately claim property tax relief
item.description:
June 23, 2026, 10 a.m. Honolulu Hale To: Honolulu City Council Committee on Budget Val Okimoto, Chair Scott Nishimoto, Vice Chair From: Grassroot Institute of Hawaii Ted Kefalas, Director of Strategic Campaigns RE: Bill 40 (2026) — RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAXATION Aloha Chair Okimoto, Vice Chair Nishimoto and other members of the Committee, The […]

The post Allow affordable housing buyers to immediately claim property tax relief appeared first on Grassroot Institute of Hawaii.
